channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Ohtani's walk-off homer boosts Dodgers to 8-0 with another comeback win
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Shohei Ohtani met the moment again, and his latest theatrics have the Los Angeles Dodgers perfect to start the season.
item.dc:creator: BETH HARRIS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: How Mesa Juniors Volleyball Club brought home Gold in Las Vegas
item.description: GRAND JUNCTION, Colo. (KREX) — Once high school volleyball season ends, several local high school athletes from all across the Western Slope come together to compete at the club level for the Mesa Juniors Volleyball Club.
item.dc:creator: Big Will Levenson, KREX 5

item.title: San Jose Giants beat Oakland Ballers in Bay Area baseball exhibition, evoking memories of original Battle of the Bay
item.description: Oakland Ballers, San Jose Giants look to fill void of lost Bay Area baseball rivalry with "Battle of the Bay 2.0" in San Jose
item.dc:creator: Christian Babcock, The Mercury News

item.title: Ovechkin scores his 892nd career goal. He needs 2 to tie Gretzky and 3 to break the NHL record
item.description: RALEIGH, N.C. (AP) — Alex Ovechkin took another step closer to breaking Wayne Gretzky's NHL record by scoring his 892nd career goal in the Washington Capitals' 5-1 loss at the Carolina Hurricanes on Wednesday night. Ovechkin moved two away from tying Gretzky and three from passing him.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: SafeSport knew of allegations against former cop before hiring him as investigator
item.description: People at the U.S. Center for SafeSport knew a former police officer was the subject of an internal investigation at his former job but hired him anyway, according to details released Wednesday by Sen. Chuck Grassley, who is looking into the matter.
item.dc:creator: EDDIE PELLS, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: NBA DEI chief Lesley Slaton stepping down: report
item.description: Lesley Slaton, the NBA's chief DEI officer, will be leaving her position, according to Adweek. The NBA will reportedly hire a replacement.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: NBA investigating Grizzles' Ja Morant, Warriors' Buddy Hield for gun-related gestures on court: report
item.description: Ja Morant has yet another gun-related investigation going on, as the league is reportedly looking into a gesture he and Warriors' Buddy Hield made on Tuesday night.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Roger Goodell suggests resolution on NFL participation in 2028 Olympics could come soon
item.description: NFL players have expressed an interest in competing in flag football at the 2028 Olympics in Los Angeles, and, according to Roger Goodell, a decision could come soon.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Eagles' celebration at Trump White House a 'good thing,' team owner says
item.description: Philadelphia Eagles team owner Jeffrey Lurie talked about what it meant to get an invite from President Donald Trump to celebrate the Super Bowl title at the White House.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Why Olivia Dunne and Paul Skenes' relationship nearly never took off
item.description: Olivia Dunne and Paul Skenes opened up about their relationship and why it nearly never got off the ground, and it all has to do with social media.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Timberwolves sale to Alex Rodriguez and Marc Lore will go forward, AP source says
item.description: The yearslong quest by Marc Lore and Alex Rodriguez to acquire control of the Minnesota Timberwolves finally seems to be in the home stretch, after current owner Glen Taylor opted not to appeal an arbitration panel's ruling that he must go forward with the $1.5 billion sale.
item.dc:creator: TIM REYNOLDS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Washington athletics org's proposals to change trans-athlete policy would violate state law, official says
item.description: Washington's high school athletics organization is unable to enact proposals that would keep biological males from girls sports because they would violate state law, an official said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: GJ Tigers Girls Soccer pours it on Central 10-0
item.description: GRAND JUNCTION, Colo. (KREX) — There was no moment to take a breath at Unity Field Tuesday night. Seemingly every few seconds yielded a breakaway chance for the Grand Junction Tigers' aggressive attack. And many of those chances were cashed in by the Tigers. Ten of them.
item.dc:creator: Big Will Levenson, KREX 5

item.title: Bucks post highest single-game field-goal percentage by any team in 21st century
item.description: MILWAUKEE (AP) — Giannis Antetokounmpo told his Milwaukee Bucks teammates they were fighting for their lives. They responded by shooting for the highest single-game percentage by any team this century.
item.dc:creator: STEVE MEGARGEE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: King fans 11 in 7-0 win against Guardians as the Padres are off to 1st 6-0 start
item.description: SAN DIEGO (AP) — Michael King struck out 11 in five innings and Jackson Merrill homered for the San Diego Padres, who started 6-0 for the first time in their 57-season history by beating the Cleveland Guardians 7-0 on Tuesday night.
item.dc:creator: BERNIE WILSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ovechkin scores No. 891. He needs 3 to tie Gretzky and 4 to pass him
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — Alex Ovechkin is a hat trick away. The Washington Capitals forward scored goal No. 891 of his career on Tuesday night, leaving him three away from tying Wayne Gretzky's NHL record and four from becoming the league's all-time leading goal-scorer.
item.dc:creator: JIMMY GOLEN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Maryland strikes quickly, hires Texas A&M's Buzz Williams after Kevin Willard's exit
item.description: Maryland moved on quickly after Kevin Willard's departure, hiring another coach with plenty of experience at a high level. Buzz Williams is changing jobs again.
item.dc:creator: NOAH TRISTER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Investigation into death of former Yankees player's son turns to carbon monoxide: reports
item.description: Investigators in Costa Rica say carbon monoxide may instead be to blame for the death of Miller Gardner, the 14-year-old son of former Yankees outfielder Brett Gardner.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, Fox 5

item.title: NFL owners meetings kick off in South Florida
item.description: With free agency still in full swing and the NFL Draft less than a month away, NFL owners, head coaches, and front office executives have made their way to The Breakers in Palm Beach for the league's annual owners meetings.
item.dc:creator: Kenny Morales, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Utes lose to Butler in College Basktball Crown
item.description: LAS VEGAS (ABC4 Sports) - The Runnin' Utes got one more opportunity to play this season, but lost to Butler in the inaugural College Basketball Crown tournament in Las Vegas Monday, 86-84. Keanu Dawes, who entered the transfer portal last week, led the Utes with 19 points and 11 rebounds.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Lee Cummard named BYU women's basketball coach
item.description: PROVO, Utah (ABC4 Sports) - BYU has named Lee Cummard as its next women's basketball head coach. Cummard has been the interim head coach since Amber Whiting resigned after three seasons on March 8.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Min Woo Lee holds off Scheffler and Woodland to win Houston Open for first PGA Tour title
item.description: Min Woo Lee kept his calm amid tremendous charges by Scottie Scheffler and Gary Woodland, winning the Houston Open on Sunday for his first PGA Tour title with the best lag of his life that set up a final par for a one-shot victory.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Houston's defense carries Cougars into 7th Final Four with 69-50 March Madness win over Tennessee
item.description: INDIANAPOLIS (AP) — Houston’s relentless, harassing defense had Tennessee's shooters dancing around. Now, the Cougars are two-stepping it back to Texas. L.J.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL MAROT, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Ilia Malinin cruises to 2nd straight title to cap an American gold rush at figure skating worlds
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — Ilia Malinin walked toward the ice for his free skate at the world championships with the swagger of a conquering hero. Then he backed up the bravado.
item.dc:creator: DAVE SKRETTA, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Madison Booker and top-seeded Texas outlast SEC rival Tennessee 67-59 to reach Elite Eight
item.description: BIRMINGHAM, Ala. (AP) — Texas coach Vic Schaefer got the tense battle he expected between his defensive-minded Longhorns and the highest-scoring team in the country in Tennessee.
item.dc:creator: ALANIS THAMES, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Fans chant 'sell the team' at Rays home opener; team owner says talks are nothing new
item.description: The Rays pulled out a win in their new home for the season at Steinbrenner Field, but the ball club's future in Tampa Bay remains uncertain.
item.dc:creator: Marilyn Parker, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Bulls face the Mavericks on 4-game win streak
item.description: Dallas Mavericks (36-38, 10th in the Western Conference) vs. Chicago Bulls (33-40, ninth in the Eastern Conference) Chicago; Saturday, 8 p.m. EDT BOTTOM LINE: Chicago aims to keep its four-game win streak intact when the Bulls take on Dallas. The Bulls are 14-22 in home games.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Former Green Bay Packer charged for alleged dogfighting
item.description: The standout college football running back played 14 games for the Green and Gold between 1994 and 1995.
item.dc:creator: Nick Witwer, NewsNation

item.title: MTS boosts Trolley service for Padres Opening Weekend
item.description: The Friar Faithful will be taking over downtown San Diego on Thursday and into the weekend as the Padres take on the Atlanta Braves, marking the first home games of the 2025 season.
item.dc:creator: Ed Lenderman, Fox 5

item.title: NCAA Tournament regionals: A look at Chicagoland connections, program history and more for the men's basketball Sweet 16
item.description: The 2025 NCAA Men's Basketball Tournament resumes Thursday night, and it's down to the Sweet 16. It'll be the Elite Eight after the regional semifinals on Thursday and Friday and then the Final Four after the regional finals on Saturday and Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Michael Johnson, WGN 9

item.title: Opening Day 2025: White Sox have a winning Opening Day record, featuring some memorable moments
item.description: CHICAGO — Opening Day is finally upon us. It's a day that comes with a sense of hope among baseball fans in early spring, a season that represents new beginnings often exemplified by the warmer temperatures and the blooming of trees and flowers.
item.dc:creator: Ethan Illers, WGN 9

item.title: White Sox Opening Day: A lot of 'new' on the South Side in 2025. What to know if you're headed to Rate Field
item.description: CHICAGO — After a 2024 season that everyone would rather forget, there's a lot of "new" on the South Side as the White Sox get set to embark on their 2025 campaign Thursday. There's a new manager, Will Venable. There's a new stadium name, Rate Field. There's a new food and beverage menu.
item.dc:creator: Michael Johnson, WGN 9
